parse.why: Results state: “No thermally induced phase transitions can be observed in the differential scanning calorimetry (DSC) curve of HCGPE, indicating that HCGPE is totally amorphous (Figure S3).” No data for BET surface area or pore volumes, and no acidity measurements are reported for HCGPE in the provided text; thus these fields are null.

For the high-concentration electrolyte (HCE), the paper provides electrochemical metrics (not included in the requested schema) but no morphology, crystal size, porosity (BET/volumes), or acidity values. Evidence: Experimental—“The high-concentration electrolyte (HCE) was prepared by dissolving a specific amount of LiFSI into DME solvent. The molar ratio of LiFSI to DME is 1.9.” Results—“According to the Arrhenius equation, the activation energy of the HCE (0.18 eV) …”; “the tLi+ of HCE (0.48)”; “HCE shows a lower onset oxidation potential compared to HCGPE.” These data do not map to morphology/crystal size/porosity/acidity fields; no BET area, pore volumes, or acid-site amounts are reported for HCE.

SEM for LAWTP powder: "This LAWTP sample shows an irregular particle morphology with particle sizes ranging from several hundred nanometers to several micrometers." No numerical crystal size, porosity (BET/volumes), or acidity values were reported for LAWTP.

For PVDF-HFP-SN, the paper reports electrochemical and thermal data (e.g., ionic conductivities 1.07×10^-5 and 3.52×10^-5 S/cm at 30 and 50 °C; DSC Tm 134.17 °C with ΔH 17.81 J/g; tLi+ = 0.296) but provides no BET surface area, pore volumes, crystal/crystallite size, or acid site quantification. SEM/cross-sectional thickness shown in Fig. 3c–e correspond to PVDF-HFP-SN-LAWTP, not PVDF-HFP-SN. Therefore morphology, porosity, crystal size, and acidity cannot be determined for PVDF-HFP-SN and are set to null.

For PVDF-HFP-SN-0.05LAWTP, the paper shows EIS/ionic conductivity trends across several LAWTP loadings (Fig. 4a–4b) but only reports explicit numeric values for the 0.1 g sample and references for pure PVDF-HFP and PVDF-HFP-SN. No specific numeric values are provided for the 0.05 g sample. No BET/porosity, acidity, crystal size, or morphology values specific to 0.05 g are given; SEM/thickness observations (Fig. 3c–e) are general for SCEs and not quantified per this composition. Therefore, all fields are set to null.

characterization.morphology: flexible membrane with LAWTP particles dispersed in PVDF-HFP matrix; non-penetrating pores observed; uniform thickness about 120 μm

parse.why: Fig. 3a-b show PVDF-HFP-SN-LAWTP solid electrolytes as flexible membranes. These descriptions apply to PVDF-HFP-SN-LAWTP samples, including the 0.1 LAWTP composition.

Text states the solid composite electrolytes are membranes: “Photographs of the PVDF-HFP-SN and PVDF-HFP-SN-LAWTP solid electrolytes.” and “the PVDF-HFP electrolyte is a semi-transparent membrane, and adding the LAWTP filler turns the membrane white.” SEM of “the PVDF-HFP-SN-LAWTP solid electrolytes” shows membrane cross-sections with uniform thickness (~120 μm), implying a membrane morphology applicable to all LAWTP-loaded SCEs, including PVDF-HFP-SN-0.2LAWTP.
